title: HF Sinclair Posts Bigger-than-Expected Loss as Refining Margins Shrink
content: Refiner HF Sinclair posted a bigger-than-expected loss for the fourth quarter, becoming the latest energy company to take a hit from weak refining margins and rising global capacity. U.S. refinery margins, measured by the 3-2-1 crack spread , averaged $16.66 in the October-December quarter, down nearly 25% from a year earlier.
author: Analysts Account
